title: Application Metadata, Transactions, Events, and Orphans · Runtime Contract: System Of Record Boundary
summary: A database row says ready before S3 accepts bytes, an object exists without an owner row after rollback, and events race ahead of scan and authorization state. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the system of record boundary increment in file metadata and object reconciliation state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Assign authoritative fields to application database, S3 object state, tags, events, or derived indexes and document reconciliation.
Defend slow bodies, range amplification, decompression, content sniffing, hot keys, tenant exhaustion, and response-header injection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict System Of Record Boundary · Theory System Of Record Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 7785 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to expose object and database crash windows and require idempotent reconciliation; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Assign authoritative fields to application database, S3 object state, tags, events, or derived indexes and document reconciliation.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the System Of Record Boundary contract for the supplied data.
